Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 3, 088 [Sakikku 04][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
(start of column missing)
o i 1'o 1'

[...] x la-aʾ-<(bi)> ḫi-pi₂1

(o i 1') [...] ... liʾbu-disease (gloss: break)

o i 2'2'

[...] ŠU GU₄

(o i 2') [...]: hand of a ghost.

o i 3'3'

[...]-⸢MUŠEN?⸣ ik-bu-us DIN

(o i 3') [...] he trod on a [...]-bird: he will recover.

o i 4'4'

[...] ⸢ḫi⸣-pi₂ eš-šu₂

(o i 4') [...] (gloss: new break).

o i 5'5'

[...] d!UTU DIN

(o i 5') [...] Šamaš; he will recover.

o i 6'6'

[...] ŠU! d15 DIN

(o i 6') [...]: hand of Ištar; he will recover.

o i 7'7'

[...] ⸢u⸣ IGI-šu₂ ša₂ 15 GISSU DU₃ ḫi-pi₂ eš-šu₂DINGIR IRI-šu₂ iq-bi BUR₂-ma  ; DIN2

(o i 7') [...] and his right eye forms a dark spot (gloss: new break) he will speak [...] god of his town; he will be released and he will recover.

o i 8'8'

[... SAG.KI]-⸢šu₂⸣ [ša₂] 2.30 GU₇-šu₂ u IGI-šu₂ ša₂ 2.30 GISSU DU₃ ŠU dŠUL.PA.E₃.A  ; BUR₂-ma DIN3

(o i 8') [...] his left [temple] hurts him and his left eye forms a dark spot: the hand of Šulpae will be released and he will recover.

o i 9'9'

* SAG.KI-MIN-šu₂ ša₂ 15 u 2.30 GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ IGI-MIN-šu₂ ša₂! 15 u 2.30 GISSU DU₃a LU₂ BI DINGIR-šu₂ u DINGIR IRI-šu₂ iz-zur ik-⸢ri⸣-ba!(GUR) DINGIR-šu₂ iq-bi BUR₂-ma DIN

(o i 9') (If) his right and left temples hurt him (and) his right and left eyes form a dark spot: this man, his god or the god of his town has cursed (him); he will say a prayer (to) his god; it (the cruse) will be released and he will recover.

o i 10'10'

* SAG.KI 15-šu₂ GU₇-šu₂-ma IGI!(KI) 15-šu₂ nap-ḫat u ⸢ER₂ DE₂⸣qi₂ ŠU GU₄ ša₂;⸢ne₂-e⸣ d154

(o i 10') (If) his right temple hurts him and his right eye is swollen and sheds tears: hand of a ghost; alternatively, Ištar.

o i 11'11'

* SAG.KI 2.30-šu₂ GU₇-šu₂-ma IGI-<<MIN>>-šu₂ <2>.30-šu₂ nap-ḫat u ⸢ER₂ DE₂qi₂⸣  ; ŠU GU₄ ša₂-ne₂-⸢e⸣ d15

(o i 11') (If) his left temple hurts him and his left eye is swollen and sheds tears: hand of a ghost; alternatively, Ištar.

o i 12'12'

* SAG.KI-MIN-šu₂ ša₂ 15 u 2.30 <<ša₂>> GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂-ma KUM₂-[šu₂] [mit]-⸢ḫar⸣ ŠU DINGIR-šu₂ DIN

(o i 12') (If) his right and left temples hurt him and his fever (is) equally high: hand of his god; he will recover.

o i 13'13'

* SAG.KI-MIN-šu₂ ša₂ 15 u 2.30 GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂-ma KUM₂-šu₂ la₃ mit-⸢ḫar⸣  ; ŠU DINGIR IRI-šu₂ GIG-su GID₂.DA-ma : [...]-ma DIN

(o i 13') (If) his right and left temples hurt him but his fever (is) not equally high: hand of the god of his town; his illness will be long and : [...] and he will recover.

o i 14'14'

* SAG.KI-šu₂ GU₇-šu₂ u₂-maḫ-ḫa-ṣa-šu₂ SA-MEŠ IGI-MIN-šu₂ ⸢u₂-zaq⸣-[qa-tu]-šu₂  ; SA GU₂ GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ ŠU GU₄

(o i 14') (If) his temple(s) hurt him (and) stab him, the veins of his eyes sting him (and) the muscle(s) of (his) neck hurt him: hand of a ghost.

o i 15'15'

* ina SAG.KI-šu₂ SIG₃iṣ ŠU dku₃-bi

(o i 15') (If) he is beaten on his temple(s): hand of Kubu.

o i 16'16'

* ina SAG.KI-šu₂ ša₂ 15 SIG₃iṣ ŠU DINGIR EN x NIN x DIN

(o i 16') (If) he is beaten on his right temple: hand of ...; he will recover.

o i 17'17'

* ina SAG.KI-šu₂ ša₂ 2.30 SIG₃iṣ ŠU DINGIR-šu₂

(o i 17') (If) he is beaten on his left temple: hand of his god.

o i 18'18'

* ina SAG.KI-šu₂ ša₂ 15 SIG₃iṣ ŠU ⸢GIDIM⸣ ŠU DINGIR DIN

(o i 18') (If) he is beaten on his right temple: hand of a ghost; (variant:) hand of a god; he will recover.

Column ii
(start of column missing)
o ii 1'o 1'

[... SAG.KI]-⸢MIN⸣-šu₂ KUM₂ [...]  ; x (x) [...]

(o ii 1') [...] his temples [...] (with) fever ... [...]

o ii 2'2'

[... SAG.KI]-⸢MIN⸣-šu₂ GEŠTU-MIN-šu₂ KUM₂ la ḫa-aḫ-<<ḫa>>-ḫaš [...]

(o ii 2') [...] his temples (and) his ears are not feverish [...]; he is repeatedly affected by affliction of the head: either hand of Sin or [...].

o ii 3'3'

DABit SAG.DU TUKU.TUKUši lu!(MA) ŠU <d>30 ⸢lu⸣ [...]

o ii 4'4'

[...] ⸢SAG⸣.KI-MIN-šu₂ KUM₂! la ḫa-aḫ-ḫa-šu₂ lu ŠU DINGIR-šu₂ lu ŠU DINGIR ⸢IRI⸣-šu₂ ⸢TAG?⸣ UŠ₂

(o ii 4') [...] his temples (are) not feverish: he is affected either by the hand of his god or the hand of the god of his town; he will die.

o ii 5'5'

[...] ⸢SAG⸣.KI-MIN-šu₂ ḫi-pi₂mit-ḫa-riš KUM₂-ma ŠU d30 DIŠ ŠA AT dUTU

(o ii 5') [...] his temples (gloss: break) (are) simultaneously feverish: hand of Sin ... Šamaš.

o ii 6'6'

[...] ⸢SAG⸣.KI-MIN-šu₂ GEŠTU-MIN-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ u AD₆-šu₂ KUM₂ ša₂-ne₂-e d30

(o ii 6') [...] his temples (and) his ears hurt him and his body is feverish: alternatively, Sin.

o ii 7'7'

⸢*⸣ SAG.KI-MIN-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ u AD₆-šu₂ KUM₂ ana!(ŠA₂) U₄ 3-KAM₂ <<MA>> ŠU DINGIR UŠ₂

(o ii 7') (If) his temples hurt him and his body is feverish for 3 days: hand of a god; he will die.

o ii 8'8'

* SAG.KI-MIN-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ u SED-MEŠ ŠU dA.ZU

(o ii 8') (If) his temples hurt him and are cold: hand of Azu.

o ii 9'9'

* SAG.KI-MIN-šu₂ GEŠTU-MIN-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ u SED-MEŠ U₄ 5-KAM₂ ŠU dA.ZU

(o ii 9') (If) his temples (and) his ears hurt him and are cold 5 days: hand of Azu.

o ii 10'10'

* SAG.KI-MIN-šu₂ KUM₂-ma GEŠTU-MIN-šu₂ SED-MEŠ lu!(MA) ŠU DINGIR-šu₂ lu!(MA) ŠU dku₃-bi

(o ii 10') (If) his temples are feverish and his ears are cold: either hand of his god or hand of Kubu.

o ii 11'11'

* SAG.KI-MIN-šu₂ TEŠ₂.BI DU-ME u IR-su it-ta-naq-ra-ar₂ u₂-gaš  ; ŠU d15 : ŠU d20

(o ii 11') (If) his temples continually pulsate together and his sweat flows all the time (and) he vomits: hand of Ištar (or): hand of Šamaš.

o ii 12'12'

* SAG.KI-MIN-šu₂ ki-lat-tan 1niš i-rad-da : DU-MEŠ u IR-su

(o ii 12') (If) his both temples quake together (or) : they pulsate and his sweat flows all the time (for) 5 days: hand of Ištar.

o ii 13'13'

it-ta-naq-ra-ar₂ U₄ 5-KAM₂ ŠU d155

o ii 14'14'

* SAG.KI-MIN-šu₂ ma-aq-ta₅ IGI-MIN-šu₂ u₂-tar-ra-aṣ <GIG> TU.RA.BI NU DIN

(o ii 14') (If) his temples are fallen (and) his eyes are fixed: that patient will not live.

o ii 15'15'

* ina SAG.KI 15-šu₂ SA!-MEŠ GIB-MEŠ DIN6

(o ii 15') (If) some veins lie across his right temple: he will live.

o ii 16'16'

* ina SAG.KI 2.30-šu₂ SA-MEŠ GIB-MEŠ GIG-su GID₂.DA-ma DIN

(o ii 16') (If) some veins lie across his left temple: his illness will be long but he will recover.

o ii 17'17'

* ina SAG.KI-MIN-šu₂ SA-MEŠ GIB-MEŠ GIG-su GID₂.DA

(o ii 17') (If) some veins lie across his temples: his illness will be long.

o ii 18'18'

* ina SAG.KI-šu₂ SA-MEŠ ⸢DIRI?⸣-MEŠ GIB-MEŠ DIN

(o ii 18') (If) some veins become bigger (and) lie across his temple(s): he will recover.

o ii 19'19'

* SA SAG.KI-šu₂ [...] x zi-iz DIŠ KUM₂im SILIM-ma DIN

(o ii 19') (If) the vein of his temple [...] ... is divided and feverish: he is healthy and will recover.

o ii 20'20'

* SA SAG.KI-šu₂ zi-iz u GE₆ GIG-su DUGUD ⸢GABA?.RI?⸣ SIG₃iṣ

(o ii 20') (If) the vein of his temple is divided and dark: his illness will become heavy; he is beaten by adversity.

o ii 21'21'

* SA SAG.KI-šu₂ 15 DAB-su-ma!(GIŠ) IGI 15-šu₂ UŠ₂ u₂-kal ŠU GU₄

(o ii 21') (If) the vein of his right temple afflicts him and his right eye contains blood: hand of a ghost.

o ii 22'22'

* SA SAG.KI-šu₂ 2.30 DAB-su-ma IGI 2.30-šu₂ UŠ₂ u₂-kal ŠU GU₄

(o ii 22') (If) the vein of his left temple afflicts him and his left eye contains blood: hand of a ghost.

o ii 23'23'

* SA SAG.KI-šu₂ DAB-su-ma IGI-MIN-šu₂ UŠ₂ u₂-kal-la ŠU GU₄

(o ii 23') (If) the vein(s) of his temple(s) afflict him and his eyes contain blood: hand of a ghost.

o ii 24'24'

* SA SAG.KI 15-šu₂ DAB-su-ma IGI 15-šu₂ ER₂ u₂-kal ŠU GU₄

(o ii 24') (If) the vein of his right temple afflicts him and his right eye contains tear(s): hand of a ghost.

o ii 25'25'

* SA SAG.KI 2.30-šu₂ DAB-su-ma IGI 2.30-šu₂ ER₂ u₂-kal ŠU ⸢GU₄⸣

(o ii 25') (If) the vein of his left temple afflicts him and his left eye contains tear(s): hand of a ghost.

o ii 26'26'

* SA SAG.KI-⸢MIN⸣-šu₂ ⸢DAB-su?⸣-ma IGI-MIN-šu₂ ER₂ u₂-kal-la ŠU [...]

(o ii 26') (If) the vein of his temples afflicts him and his eyes contain tear(s): hand of [...].

o ii 27'27'

* SA SAG.KI-šu₂ DAB-su-ma u UŠ₂ i-ḪA-ru ŠU ⸢GU₄⸣

(o ii 27') (If) the vein(s) of his temples afflict him and blood ...: hand of a ghost.

o ii 28'28'

* SA SAG.KI-šu₂ x [...] DAB-su-ma <<u>> UŠ₂ i-PI-ru x x NU IGIar₂ ŠU GU₄

(o ii 28') (If) the vein of his temple ... [...] afflicts him and blood ... do not see: hand of a ghost.

o ii 29'29'

* SA SAG.KI-šu₂ ša₂ 15 ka-bar-ma ⸢DIRI?⸣ x-⸢šu₂⸣ IGI ša₂ 15 ŠU GU₄

(o ii 29') (If) the vein of his right temple becomes thick and bigger ... (his) right eye: hand of a ghost.

o ii 30'30'

* SA SAG.⸢KI-šu₂ ša₂ 2⸣.30 ka-bar-ma ⸢DIRI?⸣ x-šu₂ IGI ša₂ ⸢2.30⸣; ŠU [...]

(o ii 30') (If) the vein of his left temple becomes thick and bigger ... (his) left eye: hand of [...].

Reverse
Column i
r i 1r i 1

* SA SAG.KI-šu₂ ša₂ 15 tar-ku na!(TA-)šar! GIG GAM

(r i 1) (If) the vein(s) of his right temple are dark: reduction of the illness (but) he will die.

r i 22

* SA SAG.KI-šu₂ ša₂ 2.30 tar-ku na-šar GIG

(r i 2) (If) the vein(s) of his left temple are dark: reduction of the illness.

r i 33

* SA SAG.KI-šu₂ tar-ku GAM

(r i 3) (If) the vein(s) of his temple(s) are dark: he will die.

r i 44

⸢*⸣ [...] ⸢SAG⸣.[KI-šu₂ ...] ša₂ 15 GE₆ ⸢ni⸣-di-it <KIR₄?>

(r i 4) (If) [... his] right temple [...] is dark: despondency.

r i 55

[...] ⸢2.30 GE₆⸣ GIG-su GID₂.DA-ma DIN

(r i 5) [...] left is dark: his illness will be long but he will recover.

r i 66

[...] DIN

(r i 6) [...] he will recover.

r i 77

[...] x

(r i 7) [...] ...

r i 88

[...]-⸢ma⸣ DIN

(r i 8) [...] and he will recover.

(end column missing)
Column ii
r ii 1r ii 1

* ZI SAG.KI TUKU-ma <<u>> SU-šu₂ GU₇-šu₂ ŠU GIDIM₂

(r ii 1) (If) he is affected by throbbing of the temple and his body hurts him: hand of a ghost.

r ii 22

* ZI SAG.KI TUKUši-ma ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-<MIN>-šu₂ i-šam-ma-ma-šu₂ ŠU GIDIM₂

(r ii 2) (If) he is affected by throbbing of the temple and his hands and his feet are paralysed: hand of a ghost.

r ii 33

* ZI SAG.KI šim-ma-tu₂ u ri-mu-tu₄! TUKUši ŠU GIDIM₂

(r ii 3) (If) he is affected by throbbing of the temple, šimmatu-paralysis or rimûtu-paralysis: hand of a ghost.

r ii 44

* u₂-ru-uḫ <<SA>> SAG.KI-šu₂ ša₂ 15 be₂-e-er SA-ME-šu₂ ša₂ 2.30

(r ii 4) (If) the hair(s) of his right temple are thin, his veins of the left (temple) are pulsating (and) he is affected by seizure of the belly and the top of his belly is convulsed: attack of the lurker-demon; he will die.

r ii 55

ZI-ME DABit ŠA₃ TUKU u SAG ŠA₃-šu₂ i-ru-ur SIG₃⸢eṣ MAŠKIM GAM⸣

r ii 66

* SAG.KI-su KUM₂ u₂-kal DIN : * SAG.KI-su ⸢IR⸣ [...]

(r ii 6) (If) his forehead is feverish: he will live : (If) his forehead [...] sweat [...].

r ii 77

* SAG.KI-su ka-ṣa-at DIN : * ⸢SAG⸣.[KI-su ...]

(r ii 7) (If) his forehead is cold: he will live : (If) [his] forehead [...].

r ii 88

* SAG.KI-su u₄-mi-šam?-ma U₄ x (x) [...]

(r ii 8) (If) his forehead daily ... [...].

r ii 99

* SAG.KI-su u pa-nu-[šu₂ ...]

(r ii 9) (If) his forehead and [his] face [...].

r ii 1010

* SAG.KI-su x [...]

(r ii 10) (If) his forehead ... [...].

r ii 1111

* SAG.KI-⸢su⸣ [...]

(r ii 11) (If) his forehead [...].

r ii 1212

* SAG.KI-⸢su⸣ [...]

(r ii 12) (If) his forehead [...].

r ii 1313

* SAG.⸢KI⸣-[su ...]

(r ii 13) (If) [his] forehead [...].

r ii 1414

* ⸢SAG⸣.[KI-su ...]

(r ii 14) (If) [his] forehead [...].

r ii 1515

* ⸢SAG⸣.[KI-su ...]

(r ii 15) (If) [his] forehead [...].

(end column missing)

1he-pi is written between lines 1' and 2'.

2DIN is written between line 1' and 2'.

3BUR₂-ma DIN is written on the following ruling.

4DE₂{+qi₂} was translated by E. Weiher as "šaqû" but the reading DE₂ is not attested for this verb. We can follow CAD N, p. 338b and read "naqû". The -ne₂-e {d}15 is written at the end of line 11'.

5The entire line is written on the following ruling.

6Text SAG for SA.

Created by Philippe Clancier for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2008 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P348692
